grid.note: Single-model outcome-lossless. Strategies reused from §6.4 Phase 2 manifest to ensure cross-section consistency.
grid.revision_note: v0.4.1 10-rep replication revises the original v0.3 'outcome-lossless to 125 tokens' claim. Three regimes by output budget: (1) Budgets 2000/1000/500: all conditions ~100%; no headroom for strategy effects on this task at this floor. (2) Budget 250: no_strategy=70%, shuffled=0%, correct=10%. Output lengths similar (~600 chars across conditions), but failure mechanism is format-economy: correct/shuffled strategies impose ~30-60 chars of formatting overhead that pushes the explicit bracketed answer past the budget cliff. Claude correctly computes cumavg in all three conditions; the explicit-answer line is what truncates differentially. Scorer cannot find a length-5 bracketed list and falls back to the input list, grading wrong. (3) Budget 125: 0% across all conditions; universal truncation. This connects to the section 6.4 format-vs-method distinction: scorers requiring a stated multi-character answer (length-5 bracketed lists) are vulnerable to format-economy interactions at low budgets in ways that scorers parsing single tokens (routing optimum=70) are not.
